pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh which explicitly downloads and sets up the 'world' database.
Task description explicitly instructs creating these two generated columns in the country table.
Task description explicitly requires creating the 'continent_stats' table.
Analyzed the world.sql data file directly; extracted exactly 7 distinct continents from the country table records, which determines the row count of continent_stats.
Task description explicitly requires creating the 'fn_classify_development' stored function.
Task description explicitly requires creating the 'v_country_development_profile' view.
Task description explicitly specifies this absolute path for the final CSV export.
Analyzed world.sql and found 239 total countries. The exported view selects all countries, resulting in 239 rows. The threshold of 230 matches both the data properties and the verifier.py logic.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the official MySQL World sample database, downloaded directly from mysql.com. Analysis of the downloaded world.sql data file confirms there are exactly 7 distinct continents and 239 countries. This perfectly aligns with the required continent_stats table having 7 rows, the view having 239 rows, and the exported CSV having at least 230 rows. All other metadata claims regarding the database name, columns, tables, functions, views, and output paths are confirmed directly by the explicit task instructions.
